content: Just as high school language teaching frames reflection as a vocabulary learning practice that builds consistent conceptual understanding over repeated practice, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to treat reflection not as a one-off error correction step, but as a cumulative vocabulary-building process for the model's internal action representations. This approach would have the LLM gradually refine its understanding of what correct reasoning 'looks like' by reinforcing consistent patterns of successful outputs across repeated self-reflection cycles, similar to how students internalize the meaning of new words through repeated reflection on their definitions and usage.
